content: Diagnosis of antibiotic resistance involves: 1) Culture and susceptibility testing (disk diffusion, broth microdilution for MIC determination); 2) Rapid molecular tests detecting resistance genes (mecA for MRSA, vanA/vanB for VRE, KPC/NDM/OXA-48 for carbapenemases); 3) Phenotypic confirmation tests (combined disk tests for ESBL, carbapenemase production); 4) MALDI-TOF MS for rapid organism identification. Interpretive breakpoints follow CLSI or EUCAST standards. Screening cultures (nasal for MRSA, rectal for VRE/CRE) identify colonization in high-risk patients. Whole genome sequencing increasingly used for outbreak investigation and resistance mechanism elucidation.
